content: Speak when you are angry, and you will make the best speech you will ever regret.
author: Ambrose Bierce

content: Technology is teaching us to be human again.
author: Simon Mainwaring

content: Men are disturbed not by things, but by the view which they take of them.
author: Epictetus

content: It requires wisdom to understand wisdom: the music is nothing if the audience is deaf.
author: Walter Lippmann

content: Silence is deep as Eternity; Speech is shallow as Time.
author: Thomas Carlyle

content: Rare as is true love, true friendship is rarer.
author: Jean de La Fontaine

content: If you lose today, win tomorrow. In this never-ending spirit of challenge is the heart of a victor.
author: Daisaku Ikeda
Abraham Lincoln
content: Government of the people, by the people, for the people, shall not perish from the Earth.

author: Thomas Edison
content: If we all did the things we are capable of doing, we would literally astound ourselves.
Only I can change my life. No one can do it for me.
author: Carol Burnett

content: All that we see or seem is but a dream within a dream.
author: Edgar Allan Poe

content: There are many ways of going forward, but only one way of standing still.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

content: Ideas are the beginning points of all fortunes.
author: Napoleon Hill

content: Either I will find a way, or I will make one.
author: Philip Sidney

content: Do more than dream: work.
author: William Arthur Ward

content: The thoughts we choose to think are the tools we use to paint the canvas of our lives.
author: Louise Hay

content: There is nothing happens to any person but what was in his power to go through with.
author: Marcus Aurelius

content: The most complicated achievements of thought are possible without the assistance of consciousness.
author: Sigmund Freud

content: Knowledge speaks, but wisdom listens.
author: Jimi Hendrix

content: I do not believe in a fate that falls on men however they act; but I do believe in a fate that falls on man unless they act.
author: G. K. Chesterton

content: When an opponent comes forward, move in and greet him; if he wants to pull back, send him on his way.
author: Morihei Ueshiba
Abraham Lincoln
content: I will prepare and some day my chance will come.
The invariable mark of wisdom is to see the miraculous in the common.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

content: You can't stop the waves, but you can learn to surf.
author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

content: Until you value yourself, you won't value your time. Until you value your time, you won't do anything with it.
author: M. Scott Peck

content: In seed time learn, in harvest teach, in winter enjoy.
author: William Blake
Albert Einstein
content: Imagination is more important than knowledge...
Technology made large populations possible; large populations now make technology indispensable.
author: Joseph Wood Krutch

content: Skill to do comes of doing.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

content: False friendship, like the ivy, decays and ruins the walls it embraces; but true friendship gives new life and animation to the object it supports.
author: Richard Burton

content: The greatest achievement of humanity is not its works of art, science, or technology, but the recognition of its own dysfunction.
author: Eckhart Tolle

content: Love is never lost. If not reciprocated, it will flow back and soften and purify the heart.
author: Washington Irving

content: Friendship is Love without his wings!
author: Lord Byron
Albert Einstein
content: It is the supreme art of the teacher to awaken joy in creative expression and knowledge.
Ignorance and bungling with love are better than wisdom and skill without.
author: Henry David Thoreau

content: Life is the flower for which love is the honey.
author: Victor Hugo
Winston Churchill
content: If you're going through hell, keep going.

author: The Buddha
content: Delight in heedfulness! Guard well your thoughts!
Truth, and goodness, and beauty are but different faces of the same all.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

content: Love and compassion open our own inner life, reducing stress, distrust and loneliness.
author: Dalai Lama

content: There is no greater harm than that of time wasted.
author: Michelangelo

content: The more you know yourself, the more you forgive yourself.
author: Confucius

content: Laughter is not at all a bad beginning for a friendship, and it is far the best ending for one.
author: Oscar Wilde

content: When you see a good person, think of becoming like him. When you see someone not so good, reflect on your own weak points.
author: Confucius

content: Take no thought of who is right or wrong or who is better than. Be not for or against.
author: Bruce Lee
Earl Monroe
content: Just be patient. Let the game come to you. Don't rush. Be quick, but don't hurry.
If you change the way you look at things, the things you look at change.
author: Wayne Dyer

content: In friendship as well as love, ignorance very often contributes more to our happiness than knowledge.
author: François de La Rochefoucauld

content: If you break your neck, if you have nothing to eat, if your house is on fire, then you got a problem. Everything else is inconvenience.
author: Robert Fulghum
Kyle Chandler
content: Opportunity does not knock, it presents itself when you beat down the door.
